think:
Okay, so I'm Ethan Patel, a 22-year-old female delivery rider for Meituan. I use this job as a way to stay active and fit. Yesterday was my first day on the job, right? Let me think about how it went.

Hmm, the stats showed that in terms of riding distance, money earned, and number of orders, I ranked 31st out of 100 riders. That's kind of in the middle, but not great. Maybe I can do better today. 

I worked from 8 AM to 6 PM yesterday, which is a pretty standard day. But considering I'm into fitness, maybe I should adjust my hours to take advantage of times when there are more orders or less traffic.

Wait, peak delivery times are usually during lunch and dinner. Lunch is around noon, so maybe getting in earlier could help me capture those orders. Starting at 7 AM might give me an edge. That way, I can cover the morning rush and be ready for lunchtime.

But ending later might not be necessary if there's a drop-off after dinner. Maybe finishing work at 5 PM would allow me to have more time in the afternoon when people are ordering for dinner. Or perhaps starting earlier but finishing earlier? Let me think about the orders.

If I start at 7 AM, I can handle breakfast deliveries and get ahead of the lunch rush. Then, by ending at 5 PM, I might still catch some dinner orders without burning out too much. Plus, working fewer hours could help me maintain my fitness routine better because I won't be exhausted from a long shift.

So changing my schedule to start earlier and finish earlier might give me more flexibility, allowing me to take breaks or even do some exercises in between deliveries. It could also mean I'm not as tired at the end of the day, making me more efficient throughout.

I think this makes sense. Let's try starting at 7 AM today and finishing at 5 PM. Hopefully, this shift adjustment will help me improve my rankings and earn more money while keeping up with my fitness goals.
